entry.title: Re: Production Order Child Item is another BoM's Parent I...

Hi,

In your scenario, you need to generate second Production Order that makes child 1, then committed qty of child 3 & 4 will be changed.

Hope this helps,

SonTran

Just wanted to know that can we deploy our own ML Python based models for advanced analytics for reporting without S4/Hana or Datasphere. Currently using Sap Analytics Cloud with BW and nothing else. 

 

Thanks

SAP Analytics Cloud Machine Learning

Hi All,

Does in C4C Auto routing is possible?

Business Case: 

If ticket is not completed in given SLA, it should auto escalate to agent's TL or any other person automatically.. is this possible. If so can any one help me

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud. How to create OData v4 service for CDS view in Web UI?
entry.content:
Hi.

I can create purchase order CDS view in Web UI, for example. SAP automatically creates OData v2 service for the view with name "CDS view name"_CDS.

Unfortunately I could not find how to create OData v4 service in Web UI.

Could anyone tell me how can I do it in Web UI, if it possible, please?

ps: when I speak Web UI, I mean this:

Anja Danneberg

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Extension Setting for File Name Output in SAP...
entry.content: just leave the filename blank and the original file name will set automatically.

entry.title: IAS as central user management system
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

If I understand correctly, it is recommended to use the IAS user store as the central user management system.

I have the following question for clarification:

I have configured Azure as the Idp and the IAS as a proxy. Using the IPS, the groups from Azure are provisioned to IAS. If I, for example, delete/modify a user in IAS, that user is re-provisioned to the IAS user store during the next IPS job. Or, for example, the users were still existing as shadow users in the BTP subaccounts, even though they were deleted in Azure AD

How is central user management in IAS configured?

Is this the recommended approach?

Many Thanks

entry.title: Re: Using SAP Signavio Process governance with Service now for ticketing and reporting
entry.content:
Hi Laura,

I am part of SAP Signavio Product Management. Can you share more details what the customer wants to achieve? Is it about requesting process changes/enhancements via ServiceNow ticket? Or triggering a workflow/case in SAP Signavio Process Governance via ServiceNow? 

Thank you and best regards

Manuel

entry.content:
hello,@PrasadAtmakuri

thanks for your sharing, I checked the revenue accounting and reporting part in our system, I didn't find the direct posting option in the IMG menu items.  we didn't implement RAR, and recently, our business scenario changes, there is a requirement to seperately recognize our revenue by event. 

our SAP S4 version is 2021 release. I want to know whether we need do some more foundamental activation RAR activities? if so, where and how to activate it! 

thanks!

entry.title: Re: Using SAP Signavio Process governance with Service no...
entry.content:
Hi Laura,

I am part of SAP Signavio Product Management. Can you share more details what the customer wants to achieve? Is it about requesting process changes/enhancements via ServiceNow ticket? Or triggering a workflow/case in SAP Signavio Process Governance via ServiceNow? 

Thank you and best regards

entry.title: Re: KNVK Custom extractor not able to identify delta records
entry.content: If a custom extractor already exists, you can use CDHDR+CDPOS to determine the actual changes and read only these elements

entry.title: planned order basic dates and production dates
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are trying to figure out the issue happening when converting planned order to production order.
Please help us with the mechanisms of calculating the basic dates between planned order and production order.

<Situation> 
There are two planned order which basic dates are as follows:
They are same material, routing, BOM, issue quantity, float(which is 0 days), production lead time (8days)
1:Planned order A
Basic start date:9/6
Basic end date:9/9

2:Planned order B
Basic start date:9/3
Basic end date:9/6

 

When we convert them to production order, the basic dates are adjusted regarding the production lead time with backwards:
1:production order A (correct calculation)
Basic start date:8/29 (8 workdays from 9/9)
Basic end date:9/9 (Same end date as planned order)

2:Production order B (weird calculation)
Basic start date:8/27 (9 workdays from 9/6)
Basic end date:9/5 (1 day earlier than planned order)


Does anyone know why production order B have calculated 9days from the basic end dates of planned order?

we have checked following attributes;
>customized calendar, and no holidays are set during these days.
>Float are all 0 days

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: How to call public cloud SAP API from embedded steamp...
entry.content:
Hello Kai,

I can not see any methods about header update for outbound delivery in below boi.

https://api.sap.com/bointerface/I_OUTBOUNDDELIVERYTP 

but API has it.

I can not update delivery item batch number in boi, it doesn't allow because it is pointed as read only field, but API can do it.

I can not post goods issue for outbound delivery in boi, but has can do it too.

entry.title: Re: Streamlining Time Sheet Approvals in SuccessFactors: Time Sheet Approval Center
entry.content:
Hi Team, 

as the other colleagues i am not able the see the Approval Center in the Action Search. If there any additional steps to activate this function? 

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Extension Setting for File Name Output in SAP...
entry.content:
Hello,

If I understand you correctly, if you leave the File Name field empty the output files are missing the extension, but can you explain why ${header.CamelFileNameOnly} is not working?

In my test scenario, ${header.CamelFileNameOnly} contains the complete path, including the filename and file extension (i.e. zip_file_name/file_name.file_extension).

Assuming that in your case ${header.CamelFileNameOnly} also only contains the file naming excluding extension, I have the following suggestions:

1: Have you tried using ${file:name}?

2: I recently had the requirement to set the target directory dynamically, which I solved by creating a map (in an exchange property) containing the file name as key and the directory as value. Perhaps something similar would also work for the file extension. Assuming that ${header.CamelFileNameOnly} only contains the the file name without extension, you could implement the following steps:

Step 1: use a Groovy script to fill the map. You can check if one of the headers or properties contains the extension type or check the file content for the correct type.

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;
import java.util.HashMap;
def Message processData(Message message) {
    
    // Retrieve properties and headers
    def properties = message.getProperties()
    def headers = message.getHeaders()

    // initiate an empty map
    def extension_map = [:]
    
    // Retrieve the filename from the headers
    def filename = headers.get("CamelFileNameOnly")

    // set the extension
    def extension = // implement logic to get file extension
    
    // If the 'Extensions' property already exists, overwrite the empty map with it
    if (properties.get("Extensions")) {
        extension_map = properties.get("Extensions")
    }

    // Append new key-value pairs
    extension_map[filename] = extension

    // Set the updated map (back) to the exchange property
    message.setProperty("Extensions", extension_map)

    return message;
}

 

Step 2:

In the File Name field, set: ${header.CamelFileNameOnly}.${property.Extensions[${header.CamelFileNameOnly}]}

I hope that one of these options works for you.

entry.title: Re: How to create Business Users in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Can anybody explain why the BUP003 and BUP010 roles would not be created in the business partners?  My relationship analysis looks the same except the roles section is missing for both business partners.

I had to copy the BUP003 and BUP010 roles from client 000, I'm worried I missed something during the copy.

entry.title: Re: Request ID in Replication Flows
entry.content:
Hi Tobias, 

I would create a custom table, with the 'flow ID' and timestamp as keys. 'request ID' should be system-wide unique so I would concat the flow ID and timestamp. 

In a task chain: 

1.  Update the custom table with a transformation flow -> enter the flow ID and time stamp

2. Load the data into a 'load' table 

3. use View to move from 'load' table to 'consumption' table and add the 'request' ID using SQL 

 

I see the overhead in tracking / creating the 'request' ID. You will need to have a custom transformation flow for each load. It would also be possible to omit the step 2 and load directly into a 'consumption' table, but you need to use SQL. Python is too slow. 

Cheers

Martin

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello David,

I hope this message finds you well. My name is Alice Mazzone, and I’ve been working as a Product Consultant for the past years. I’m reaching out regarding the certification opportunity, which I believe aligns perfectly with my background and aspirations.

As a woman and a first-generation college graduate, I understand the importance of breaking through barriers and seeking out opportunities that may not always be easily accessible, especially for individuals in remote or underserved areas. Coming from a minority background, I’ve seen firsthand how these factors can impact access to resources and economic opportunities, and it has only strengthened my resolve to push forward.

Working with SAP has been incredibly rewarding for me, as it enables businesses to optimize their processes and achieve better outcomes through technology. I’m constantly amazed at the impact we can make, and I’m passionate about continuing to expand my knowledge and skills in this field.

This certification would be a significant step in my professional growth, allowing me to deepen my expertise and stay current with evolving technologies. I am very excited about the potential it holds and appreciate the chance to apply for the free certification attempt.

Thank you for considering my application. I’m enthusiastic about the journey ahead and the continued opportunities to learn and contribute.

Best regards,

Alice.

Unfortunately that does happen, the local API and public API not being entirely in sync (yet). In that case I would just call the public API... because why not? 🙂

Regards,
Egbert

NWBC struggles heavily because of dealing with a lot of FPM events and loading tons of data in each step. 

Regards,
Marcel

entry.content:

entry.title: Re: How to enable translation keys on Android
entry.content:
1. Logon with account name 
2. Enter 
3. Go to settings.. Enable translation keys

entry.title: Functional differences between BOI's and API's
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I need to update some header fields of outbound delivery but BOI has not functionality about it.

https://api.sap.com/bointerface/I_OUTBOUNDDELIVERYTP 

But on the other hand, api can make it...

Actually this is not only difference between them.

You can not update outbound delivery item' s batch by using boi but api can do.

You can not post goods issue by using boi but api can do...

Why there is differences between boi and api?  

Am I missing something? Maybe I can not find correct boi for my requirements.

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Creating an SAP Account (SAP Universal ID) and getting started in the community
entry.content: I am Interested in SAP Digital Skills. Please provide Invitation

entry.title: Re: ABAP Test Cockpit in the Cloud – How to use baseline
entry.content:
Hi,

Thank you for your detailed blogs about ATC.

We are an On Premise S/4 Hana 2022, and starting our journey to clean core. We are planning to activate Abap Cloud and ATC variant for cloud readiness.

As we have a ton of legacy code, we are using exemption so that only new code is checked versus new Abap rules, to start with.

Nevertheless, we want to correct, over time, our legacy code, to make it abap cloud compliant.

Is there a possibility to exclude objects from an already defined baseline, so that we can work on them and have the relevant findings we would need to correct on these objects to make them cloud compliant ? 

Our use case : use boy scout and clean islands concepts as explained in https://github.com/SAP/styleguides/blob/main/clean-abap/CleanABAP.md#how-to-get-started-with-clean-code, to start and improve our existing code over time.

Thank you for your input

entry.title: Unlock Your Potential with the New SAP Cloud ALM Solution Transformation Consultant Training
entry.content:
As digital transformation accelerates, SAP Cloud ALM stands out as a vital tool for effective application lifecycle management. We are excited to announce our new Solution Transformation Consultant Training program, designed specifically for SAP professionals ready to deepen their expertise and lead transformative initiatives. This program will equip you with the latest strategies and hands-on skills to navigate comprehensive solution transformations within your organization.

Why Solution Transformation Matters

Solution transformations are vital for SAP professionals in today’s fast-paced environment. By focusing on solution transformation, SAP professionals can directly support their organizations' strategic goals. This approach enables teams to implement SAP's cloud solutions more efficiently, driving business innovation and adaptability in a competitive landscape.

What Awaits You in the Training Program

This training program equips you with practical skills and knowledge to effectively lead transformation projects while offering the flexibility needed for SAP professionals.

Key Learning Objectives

Participants will:

  • Gain Insight into Business Transformation: Understand SAP's comprehensive business transformation strategy and explore the integrated tool chain.
  • Navigate the Transformation Journey: Learn the process from agile implementation to efficient operations within SAP Cloud ALM.
  • Acquire Hands-On Experience: Develop practical skills in project planning, task management, solution documentation, test orchestration, and deployment.
  • Connect Learnings to RISE with SAP: Discover how SAP Cloud ALM supports the RISE with SAP methodology, enhancing transformation initiatives.
  • Comprehend Transition Strategies for SAP Cloud ALM: Understand SAP's shift from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM, preparing for future advancements.

 

Embrace a Flexible Learning Journey

Our Solution Transformation Consultant Training is designed with a flexible learning approach to meet the needs of busy professionals:

  • Self-Paced Learning Journey: Engage in a comprehensive self-paced journey titled Transforming for Success: Solution Transformation with SAP Cloud ALM allowing you to learn at your own speed.
  • ALM Events Participation: Join key events such as the ALM Summit EMEA, the ALM Partner Roadshows, Deep-dive Workshops, etc.. These sessions provide mandatory and recommended insights to enhance your understanding and stay updated on the latest developments.
  • Continued Learning Opportunities: Stay informed about ongoing SAP Cloud ALM learning offerings through the SAP Cloud ALM Learning Portal, ensuring you remain at the forefront of solution transformations.

Elevate Your Career with Knowledge Badge and Certification

We offer two levels of recognition for your expertise in Solution Transformation knowledge:

  1. Knowledge Badge: **Transformation Knowledge 2024 - Solution Transformation Consultant** 

This badge certifies your understanding of the transformation approach and the latest updates at a beginner level. You can earn this badge by completing self-paced learning, attending related sessions during respective ALM events, and passing the assessment of the Solution Transformation Consultant Training.

  1. SAP Certificate: **SAP Certified Associate - Solution Transformation Consultant with SAP Cloud ALM** 

Challenge yourself by taking the certification exam for STC. This credential enhances your professional reputation and career prospects, proving your comprehensive understanding and in-depth skills as a solution transformation consultant. It equips you to lead project teams through the solution transformation journey using SAP Cloud ALM. Be sure to follow the structured learning path: Transforming for Success: Solution Transformation with SAP Cloud ALM. This journey will guide you through essential learning experiences that ensure your success.

Ready to Take the Leap?

Don’t hesitate—jump in today! For details of this training program, visit the Solution Transformation Consultant Training Infopage. By joining this training, you're not just expanding your knowledge; you're preparing to make a meaningful impact in the digital transformation landscape. Embrace this chance to elevate your career!

Engage with Us!

We want to hear from you! Drop your questions or feedback in the comments below and connect with your fellow professionals. For any inquiries about the training program, reach out to cloudalm@sap.com.

entry.title: Converting dimensional Attributes into Fact Measures
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

is it possible to transform dimensional Attributes into Fact Measures without joining them in into the Fact table ?

entry.title: Re: Spartacus B2B Punchout Level1 Implementation
entry.content:
I tried with more recent SAP Commerce and Spartacus (2211.25) and it mostly works with a few code  modifications but then I hit an issue adding items to the cart. This was due to  the b2bPunchOutAllowedUrlList in the b2bopunchoutocc extension needing  /{baseSiteId}/users/{userId}/carts url adding. Also, the b2bopunchoutocc extension is expecting the sid parameter as a header in cart OCC calls.

Did the followup blog covering how you can send the cart details back to the procurement system get published ?

Tried in cognito mode as well. But still it says -Sorry, thats not quite right. I am left only with this answer, all of them has finished.. Not sure of the issue here..May be lot of attempts, with wrong answer kiccked me out of the execuse in Build apps 🙂

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Foreign Currency Remeasurement for General Ledger Accounts in SAP Business ByDesign
entry.content:
hello @katrin_gruber , 

I hope you are able to help to let me understand the behavior of the Foreign Currency Remeasurement run for the following case.

I use the OASSET and OLIAB G/L to book some accrued revenue or accrued costs in foreign currencies. 

E.g. My set of books is in EUR 

On 01/09/2024 I booked 1000 GBP with a FX rate 1 GBP to 1,10 EUR on the G/L "130000 - Other accruals" of type  OASSET. 

DR. "130000 - Other accruals" 1100 EUR  (1000 GBP) 
CR. "400100 - Other income"   1100 EUR  (1000 GBP) 

On 25/09/2024 I booked 1000 GBP with a FX rate of 1 GBP to 1,15 EUR 

DR. "110000 - Receivables"     1150 EUR (1000 GBP) 
CR. "130000 - Other accruals" 1150 EUR (1000 GBP) 

On 30/09/2024, on the G/L "130000 - Other accruals" my balance in GBP is 0,00 GBP. However, if I run the Foreign currency remeasurement run for 009/2024 for currency GBP, the system will calculate a not realized gain of 50 EUR. Why does the system remeasures my balance GBP balance even if it 0,00 ? 

thank you

entry.title: Re: How to call public cloud SAP API from embedded steamp...

The coding isn't the issue, you get an example from SAP for the CRUD operations as soon as you generate the Service Consumption Model.

entry.title: Issue with Android Emulator
entry.content: I am encountering an error when trying to run my NativeScript application on an Android emulator using Visual Studio Code. The command I am using is: tns run android –device emulator-5554.

entry.title: Re: How to call public cloud SAP API from embedded steamp...
entry.content: Thanks 🙂

entry.title: Re: VIES (VAT validation via EU Commission Web Service) with SAP Master Data Governance: MDG-BP/Cust
entry.content:
Hi @saikatjay , 

thanks a lot for sharing the info about OVS VIES integration into MDG!

With OVS there's not only VIES integration, but several others where SAP is delivering a standard configuration (and the option for customer-specific validations)...

  • Bank accounts against the List of VAT Payers owned by the Polish tax authorities 
  • Bank accounts against the Slovakian Bank Accounts Register
  • Unreliable payer against the register of the Tax Office of the Czech Republic
  • Customer-specific validation

I've created a new Continious Influence Request to integrated above checks / OVS in general into MDG:

"MDG - Integrate SAP (OVS Online Validation System) into Central Governance"
https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/330804

entry.title: Quality management quarantine time available in public cloud
entry.content: HI, Is it possible to get quarantine time available also to public cloud. Working in customer project where they would need certain time freeze between two production steps. Semifinished items would be in stock for example 24 hours before they could be used in next production step. In QM side no possibility to add group 03 time reference, due public cloud.

entry.title: Re: sap.m.feedInput control error
entry.content: The error log caused by sap.m.FeedInput is addressed in https://github.com/SAP/openui5/issues/4047. If you have a GH account, click on "Subscribe" there to get notified when there is an update.

entry.title: Re: Scheluding exam problem
entry.content:
Hi @berk_abay 
In order to provide individual support please create ticket in our User Support Center 
To help the support team to understand your query better, could you please provide the following information in your correspondence?

  •            Detailed description of the issue
  •            Screenshots: To help us visualize the issue
  •            Please also provide the Course Code of course.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Build Queries using CDS Analytical Projection Views in Eclipse
entry.content:
Hi Mani,

The filter defined in @Consumption.filter on a field like posting date is passed to table level to filter data in HANA. Therefore it is always a good idea to filter data in CDS query to get a better performance.

Best regards,

Giselle

entry.title: Re: Comparison of SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office (SAC edition) vs. SAC add-in for Microsoft Excel
entry.content:
@SteffenHeine 

Thanks for the overview,

From an end user perspective to choose the right tool for own Excel workbooks more details should be mentioned, like:

In SAC AddIn there is no overview of available Dimensions like in AfO (and BEx) in the navigation panel
In SAC AddIn you do not have a context menu with right mouse click
In SAC AddIn you cannot choose between short, medium and long text descriptions

Kind Regards

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: SAP SLT Replicate only New Records ?
entry.content:

Can you please let me know if we can Replicate only new records into Target HANADB from SAP ECC using SLT?

I want to load only new records and exclude or filter out delta records for the same key.

Can we configure SAP SLT like this ? For example adding New generic field as  timestamp field as KEY in LTRS ? i tried this but cannot mark the field as KEY.

entry.title: Re: Request ID in Replication Flows

entry.title: Re: Legal Right to use RFC component
entry.content: Yes, I also think that this question would have to be answered by the third-party, which developed these function modules. SAP does not possess any ownership/copyright of software developed by another company.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
Re: Adding BP Code (where applicable) to a SAP B1 query
entry.content: You can't have multiple joins on the same column T0."TransType" , even if you're adding another condition , to do so , you need to go for "union all" , do a query for every join and then link them using Union All

entry.title: Re: OSS Reporting in Public Cloud
entry.content: Thank you!

entry.title: SAP Cloud Integration: Advanced Event Mesh Adapter: OAuth [6]: using Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AAD)
entry.content:
SAP Cloud Integration (aka CPI) offers an “Advanced Event Mesh Adapter” which is well integrated with the “Advanced Event Mesh” broker.
This article shows how to set up a scenario where we send a message from iFlow via “Advanced Event Mesh Adapter” to SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh (AEM) with OAuth Authentication.
This tutorial is based on Microsoft Entra ID (Azure) and focuses on "Client Credentials" flow.
You might be also interested in authorization code flow with IAS and client credentials with XSUAA or authorization code with XSUAA.

Technologies covered in this blog post:

  • SAP Business Technology Platform, Cloud Foundry environment (BTP)
  • SAP Cloud Integration (CPI)
  • SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh (AEM)
  • OAuth 2.0, Microsoft Entra ID (Azure)

Content

0. Introduction
1. Entra ID: Create OAuth Client
2. AEM: Configure OAuth Settings
3. CPI: Create iFlow
4. Run Scenario

Prerequisites

  • To follow this tutorial, access to  SAP Advanced Event Mesh is required.
  • Access to Microsoft Azure with admin permissions 
  • Access to the previous tutorials of this series
  • I recommend bookmarking the Security Glossary

0. Introduction

We want to send a message from an iFlow to Advanced Event Mesh Broker.
We want to use the Advanced Event Mesh adapter.
Authentication should be configured with OAuth 2.0 client credentials.
OAuth authorization should be provided by Microsoft Entra ID (Azure).

This blog post is a sibling of this previous tutorial which was based on XSUAA:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-cloud-integration-advanced-event-mesh-adapter-oauth-authentication/ba-p/13704534

Please head over to that blog post for detailed introduction.

Below diagram shows our setup:

We can see that the OAuth client created in Entra ID (App registration) is configured in CPI and in AEM.
The required JWT token is fetched by the security artifact in CPI.

In the following hands-on tutorial, we’re going through the following steps:

- Register an OAuth client at the Authorization Server (Entra ID).
- Configure the Resource Server (AEM) to support OAuth.
- Configure the client application to use the OAuth client with “client credentials” flow (CPI).

1. Entra ID: Create OAuth Client

In the OAuth model, a web application is a "client" that acts on behalf of a user, without getting in touch with the user's password. That's taken care of by the "Authorization Server", in our scenario it is provided by Entra ID. In order to communicate with the Authorization Server, the client needs credentials. Therefore, it must be "registered" at the Authorization Server.
In Entra ID, creating an OAuth client is called: create a new "App Registration".
The OAuth flow "client credentials" is a kind of shortcut that doesn't require user interaction, thus suitable for app to app scenarios. 

To access Microsoft Azure portal, we go to
https://portal.azure.com
and login with an admin user (can be a trial admin).

1.1. Create App Registration

We go to Azure Portal -> "Entra ID" -> "Manage" -> "App registrations" -> “New registration”.
We enter the following data:

Name:
Enter any name of your choice.
In my example: "AppForAEM".

Account Type:
Single Tenant (default).

Redirect:
Not required for client-credentials scenario.

Press “Register”.
We’re taken to the overview page where we can view the details.
We take a note of the application ID, which is the OAuth client ID.
In my example:
Client ID: 97ad5739-9d18

1.2. Endpoints

We need to know the URL of the authorization server in order to request a JWT token.
On the top menu, we press on “Endpoints”.

We take a note of the following 2 endpoints:
1) "OAuth 2.0 token endpoint (v2)".
E.g.

 

 

 

 

 

https://login.microsoftonline.com/111/oauth2/v2.0/token

 

 

 

 

 

2) "OpenID Connect metadata document"
E.g. 

 

 

 

 

 

https://login.microsoftonline.com/111/v2.0/.well-known/openid-configuration

 

 

 

 

 

1.3. Configure App Registration: Define Credentials

We cannot use the new OAuth client without credentials.
So we create the client secret now.
In the screen of our created App registration, we click on "Manage" -> "Certificates & secrets " in the navigation pane.
Then click on "New client secret".
We enter any description and press “Add”.

Afterwards we should not forget to take a note of the secret, as it won’t be shown anymore.
Note:
In case you lost your secret, you can always create and use a new one.

Recap

The configuration required in Microsoft Entra ID comprise:

  • Create application
  • create secret

2. AEM: Configure OAuth Authentication

Now we head over to AEM for defining the configurations required for OAuth authentication.
Our iFlow will establish a connection to AEM and will have to authenticate.
Instead of authenticating with username and password, we want to use OAuth.
As a consequence, AEM will receive a JWT token.
Which means that AEM will have to somehow check if the token can be accepted.
Below configurations enables AEM to do this validation.

At AEM, 3 steps are required:

- Enable OAuth (only once)
- Create OAuth Profile (for each OAuth client)
- Set default profile (in case of multiple profiles)

2.1. Optional: Enable OAuth Authentication

We login to AEM, click on Cluster Manager, choose our broker and click on the "Manage" tab.
In the section "Event Broker Service Settings" we click on "Authentication".
We enable OAuth Provider Authentication and save.

This step needs to be executed only once.

2.2. Create OAuth Profile

In Cluster Manager, we choose our broker and click on "Open Broker Manager".
There, we click on "Messaging" -> "Access Control" on the left navigation pane.
Below "Client Authentication" tab, we go to the sub-tab "OAuth Profiles".

Settings

Name
Any name of your choice, in my example "EntraCliCre".
After pressing "Create" we can start to configure this new profile.
Note:
One profile corresponds to one OAuth client.
In our case, the OAuth client is an “App Registration” in Entra ID, but it could be as well an XSUAA service instance or an "Application" in IAS, etc

Enabled
yes

OAuth Client ID
Here we enter the clientid which we copied in chapter 1.1.
In my example, I enter 97ad5739-9d18

Change OAuth Client Secret
We should not forget to click here, although it doesn’t seem to be a field. 
Here we enter the clientsecret which we copied in chapter 1.3.

OAuth Role
This is a confusing setting.
Anyways, in case of OAuth flow, we choose Resource Server.
It makes sense, because in the OAuth diagram we assigned the role of Resource Server to AEM.
With other words, this is the setting for the “client-credentials” scenario.

Issuer Identifier
Another confusing field.
We can enter any string of our choice, as long as we use it when sending a request to AEM.
This value is an ID which helps AEM to find the proper OAuth profile for an incoming JWT token.
With other words:
At the end of the day, we send a JWT token to AEM.
AEM contains multiple OAuth profiles.
So how does AEM know, which profile to use for validating the incoming token?
The answer is easy to guess:
It uses this field .
First of all, the validator reads the issuer which is carried by the JWT (the property name inside JWT token is iss).
And it searches for a profile with the same Issuer Identifier.
Alternatively, we can enter any unique string in this field.
In that case, we have to send this same string in the request, as a special property.
(Documentation can be found here)

In our tutorial, we use the Entra ID issuer for our tenant.
This is the value of the iss claim in the JWT token
We use the url property from chapter 1.2 and adapt it as follows:
https://sts.windows.net/7d9a2f86.../

Discovery Endpoint
This is required to look up other endpoints like the JWKS and introspection endpoints, required for validating the incoming JWT token.
In my example:
https://login.microsoftonline.com/7d9a2f86/v2.0/.well-known/openid-configuration

JWKS Endpoint
It is not required in our scenario

Introspection Endpoint
not available at Entra ID

Authorization Groups Claim Name
We leave this empty.

Username Claim Name
In our tutorial we use: "appid"
This is the claim which contains the clientid (same as application id at Entra ID)

Parse Access Token
This is an important setting, because Entra ID doesn't have introspection endpoint.
We have to set it to enabled.

Validate Access Token Audience
The aud claim in a JWT token contains a list of receivers that are intended by this token.
The client itself is always in the aud.
As such, we can enable the setting and enter the clientid which we received in chapter 1.2

Validate Access Token Issuer
We can enable this validation and enter the exact issuer which is contained in the iss claim of the JWT token.
In my example:
https://sts.windows.net/7d9a2f86/

Validate Access Token Scope 
We disable this validation

Validate Access Token Type
We can enable this validation and limit to "jwt".

  Finally, we can press “Apply” in the right upper corner.

2.3. Configure Default Profile

After creating an OAuth profile, it is ready to be used.
We've seen in the AEM dashboard that the new profile is added to a list of multiple OAuth profiles.
Now there's another setting in the "Client Authentication" Tab which allows to define which of the existing profiles should be used as default.

Within "Broker Manager" we go to
Access Control -> Client Authentication -> Settings
and press "Edit"
We make sure that "OAuth Authentication" is enabled.
Now we select our OAuth Profile which we created above as Default Profile Name from the drop-down.

And as usual:
Finally, we press “Apply”.

This step is not required if we define the exact issuer name in the "issuer identifier" field in the profile.

2.4. Create Username

AEM requires a user context for messaging scenario.
Otherwise we get an error "403 Client Username Is Shutdown".
In our tutorial, we use the clientid as username.
We go to Broker Manager -> Messaging -> Access Control -> Client Usernames
Create new username with the clientID copied above.
We "Enable" the user and press "Apply".

2.5. Retrieve Connection details

To view the data that we need to enter in the next chapter, we go to
Cluster Manager -> Select Broker -> Connect
We open any language and choose smf-Protocol -> Get Started
We need the host and the VPN name.
In my example:
Host URI: "tcps://mr-connection-abc.messaging.solace.cloud:55443"
Message VPN: "demovpn"

Optional: View Port Config

One optional last check, to be on the safe side:
Cluster Manager -> Broker -> Manage -> Advanced Options -> Port Configuration -> Expand Public Endpoint -> click “Edit”
Expand “Solace Messaging”
Make sure that the “Secured SMF Host” is enabled and the port number matches the above: 55443

 

2.6. Create Queue

If not yet available, we create a queue to which we’ll send events from iFlow.
To do so, we go to
Broker Manager -> Messaging -> Queues
and create a queue with name “testqueue”.

Recap

At AEM, we’ve configured OAuth via these steps:

Broker Service
- optional: enable OAuth Authentication
Broker Manager
- create OAuth Profile
- optional: set default OAuth Profile
- create username from clientID

3. CPI: Create iFlow

At this point, the AEM broker is ready to receive JWT tokens issued for the OAuth client created in chapter 1.
Now we head over to Cloud Integration dashboard where 2 tasks are waiting for us:
- create a Security Artifact for handling the JWT token
- create an iFlow with AEM adapter for sending messages

3.1. Create Security Artifact

We create a security artifact for securely storing the credentials which we received in chapter 1.2.
CPI provides a special artifact type for OAuth 2 which is able to automatically fetch a JWT token with “Client Credentials” flow.
We go to Monitor -> Security Material
and click on Create -> OAuth 2 Client Credentials

In the dialog, we enter the following values:

Name
Any name of our choice.
Note that this name will be used in the iFlow.
In my example, I’m entering "EntraCliCre".

Token Service URL
This is the endpoint provided by the Authorization Server, to be used for requesting a JWT token.
We enter the value of the url property of chapter 1.2.
The full URL in my example:
https://login.microsoftonline.com/7d9a2f86/oauth2/v2.0/token 

Client ID
Here we enter the value of the clientid property which we copied in chapter 1.1.
In my example, I enter "97ad5739-9d18"

Client Secret
Here we enter the clientsecret property which we copied in chapter 1.3.

Client Authentication
We choose “Send as Body Parameter”.

Scope
Entra ID requires a scope parameter, to be composed in a specific way:
<clientid>/.default
In my example: 97ad5739-9d18/.default

Content-Type
We choose “application/x-www-form-urlencoded"

 

Finally, we press "Deploy".

 

With this, we’ve enabled CPI to fetch a JWT token for us.
Note that the credentials have to be the same as we’ve configured in the AEM profile.

3.2. Create iFlow

Now we can go ahead and create the iFlow.
In this tutorial the iFlow is as minimalistic as possible, as we only want to show the usage of the AEM adapter.

We create a simple iFlow which does really nothing but sending messages to the Event Broker.
We define a hard-coded dummy message text.
We just want to make sure that the OAuth authentication works fine.
Let's quickly go through the steps:

Start Event
The iFlow is triggered once after deploy, by a "Timer" start event.

Content Modifier
In the "Message Body" tab we enter any dummy message text of our choice.

AEM Adapter: Connection tab
"Host":
The value for "host" is copied from AEM and pasted as is, no need to cut or append anything.
We've copied that value in chapter 2.5
In my example: "tcps://mr-connection-abc.messaging.solace.cloud:55443"

"Message VPN":
Same as above, the value of chapter 2.5.
In my example: "demovpn"

"Username":
We can enter the clientid, which was created as username.

"Authentication Type":
Select “OAuth2”.

"OAuth2 Credential Type":
Choose "OAuth2 Client Credentials".

"OAuth2 Client Credentials Credential Name":
Here we enter the name of the security artifact created in chapter 3.1.
In my example: "EntraCliCre".

AEM Adapter: Processing tab
Here we configure our queue.

"Endpoint Type":
We select "Queue".

"Destination Name":
Here we enter the name of the desired target queue.
In my example: “testqueue”.

That’s it for iFlow creation.
BTW, saving is always a good idea.

4. Run Scenario

Now we can deploy the iFlow and check the result:

In CPI :
The log at “Monitor Message Processing” should show success message:

In AEM:
We go to “Broker Manager” and check the number of “Messages Queued” which should have increased.

Consume:
Another option would be to use the “Try-Me” tool in AEM to consume the messages sent from iFlow.
We go to Cluster Manager -> Select Service -> Connect
We expand "Solace Web Messaging" and copy the “Connection Details”.
Then we go to "Broker Manager" -> Try Me 
and enter the connection details and the queue name.
Immediately, we receive the messages and can check the dummy content which we specified in the “Content Modifier” step of the iFlow.

With that we can be happy.
We've confirmed that OAuth authentication can be realized with CPI and AEM via AEM adapter and Microsoft Entra ID

Troubleshooting

See Troubleshooting blog post for hints and detailed description about how to access the AEM logs.

Sample Content of JWT token

Issued by Microsoft Entra ID via client credentials:

 

 

 

{
   "sub":"3a856797-c1a0",
   "aud":"97ad5739-9d18",
   "iss":"https://sts.windows.net/7d9a2f86/",
   "appid":"97ad5739-9d18",
   "appidacr":"1",
   "idp":"https://sts.windows.net/7d9a2f86/",
   "oid":"3a856797-c1a0-476d",
   "rh":"0.AXoAhi-afVzwf0AA.",
   "tid":"7d9a2f86-f05c-407f",
   "uti":"7_PAy2XxuUKy",
   "iat":1727264365,
   "nbf":1727264365,
   "exp":1727268265,
   "aio":"E2dYltAA==",
   "ver":"1.0"
}

 

Summary

In this blog post we've learned how to use OAuth authentication for sending messages from iFlow via AEM adapter.
We've learned that 3 configuration steps are required at AEM:

AEM Broker Service: enable OAuth Authentication
AEM Broker Manager: create OAuth Profile
AEM Broker Manager: define default OAuth Profile

And we've learned how to configure the AEM adapter for an iFlow receiver channel:
CPI Monitoring: Create security artifact for "Client Credentials"
CPI iFlow AEM adapter: use the security artifact for OAuth2 authentication.
CPI iFlow AEM adapter: Connection details are copied from "Connection" tab in AEM.

In this blog post we've used Microsoft Entra ID as Authorization Server.
We've focused on the OAuth flow "Client Credentials".

Key Takeaways

AEM: the profile has to be configured with:

  • "Parse Access Token": true  because there’s no introspection endpoint
  • "Username Claim Name": appid

AEM: create Username with clientid
CPI: security materials needs scope parameter

Links

OAuth spec https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749
AEM docu https://help.pubsub.em.services.cloud.sap/Cloud/cloud-lp.htm
Microsoft Entra docu landing page

Blog: AEM Adapter with client credentials with IAS
Blog: AEM Adapter with client credentials with XSUAA
Blog: AEM Adapter with client credentials with Entra ID: this blog
Blog: AEM Adapter with authorization code with XSUAA
Blog: AEM Adapter with authorization code with IAS
Blog: AEM Adapter with authorization code with Entra ID
Blog: AEM Adapter with client certificate
Blog: Troubleshooting AEM

Appendix: Node.js sample code

Please find below some Node.js script that I've used to fetch a JWT token from Entra ID.
Make sure to adapt the values.

 

const axios = require('axios')

//ENTRA ID
const CLIENTID = "97ad5739-9d18"
const SECRET = "sHH8Q~ZtZRUxU8d"
const URL_TOKEN_FULL = "https://login.microsoftonline.com/7d9a2f86/oauth2/v2.0/token"


/* HELPERS */

async function fetchJwtToken(clientid, clientsecret, tokenurl){
    const auth = Buffer.from(clientid + ':' + clientsecret).toString("base64");
    const options = {
        url: tokenurl ,
        headers: {
            'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded',
            'Authorization': 'Basic ' + auth        
        },
        method: 'POST',
        // in case of ENTRAID we need to send scope param:
        data: `grant_type=client_credentials&response_type=token&client_id=${clientid}&scope=${clientid}/.default`
    }
    const response = await axios(options)
    return response.data.access_token
}

async function decode(jwt){
    let jwtBase64Encoded = jwt.split('.')[1];
    let jwtDecoded = Buffer.from(jwtBase64Encoded, 'base64').toString('ascii');
    console.log('===> JWT: ' + jwtDecoded);
    let jwtDecodedJson = JSON.parse(jwtDecoded);
    console.log('===> JWT: client_id: ' + jwtDecodedJson.client_id);
    console.log('===> JWT: issuer: ' + jwtDecodedJson.iss);
}


/* SCRIPT */

async function run(){
    // fetch JWT 
    const jwt = await fetchJwtToken(CLIENTID, SECRET, `${URL_TOKEN_FULL}`)
    console.log("Fetched JWT token: " + (jwt != undefined))
    decode(jwt)    
}

run()

 

🍀

entry.title: Re: Adding BP Code (where applicable) to a SAP B1 query
entry.content: By the way, the condition next to each join has no meaning and might be raising syntax error

entry.title: Re: No alternative BOM field in STPO
entry.content: It is helpful , i have the same issue for some of the case i did found the Alt. Bom value then we can get it from STAS table, my issue resolve. Thanks

entry.title: Re: Scheluding exam problem
entry.content: Hi @Margit_Wagner I solved my problem quickly thanks to your support team, thank you very much.

entry.title: Digital Skills Initiative
entry.content: I am interested in digital skills certification with more than three years of experience in SAP, as I find it a reasonable career path where I find many opportunities to explore and learn new things everyday.

entry.title: SAP Business ByDesign WoC Organizational Management: Quick Edit
entry.content:
Organizational structures of entities could be complex, even for SME companies. If you just need to adjust e. g. an org. unit address within a (consistent) organizational structure, you can use the "Quick Edit" button in WoC view Org List View (as shown on the screen shot below): 

Execute your changes - Save & Close.

This could reduce you editing time. Please try it out in your test tenant (first).

entry.title: Re: Start Your scripting Journey The Easy Way With SAP Analytics Cloud | Part Two
entry.content: @nguyen_thanh_hieu  should not be a problem but I have found that it can be awkward - you need to do a select all (Ctrl-A) to select all the text in the text box before changing the size - that will do it. It's the dynamic text items that seem to have the issues most.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
entry.content: Me and Bunji coding doing some old school ABAP

entry.title: Re: SAPCAR GUI
entry.content: @Sandra_Rossi , @Eric  - links restored!

entry.title: Bank Account Import from Import/Export Bank Account inS4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

My Client has many bank accounts. So, they cannot create manually. We followed the note (https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2985113 ) as well. When import bank account from Import/Export Bank Account in Manage Bank Account, the error shows as "House bank UOBSG with bank key SG UOBSG does not exist in company code 1000". We thought that this error came up because of House bank is needed to be created. In the system, we have only "Display House Banks" app and creating house bank is directly connected with "Manage Bank Accounts" App. 

Thus, pls kindly give an advice to proceed bank account importing process. Thanks in advance.

Best Regards,

Hnin

entry.title: Re: Performance Issue with /SCWM/TO_CONFIRM in SAP EWM
entry.content: thanks for comment.

entry.title: My Compliance check results
entry.content:
Information required on My compliance Check results.

1.How data is flowing toward the checks in the My compliance Check results, is there any program or process to be complied?

2.for creating new check for any scenarios in My compliance Check results, how it can be done?

entry.title: Shipment Cost SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear experts,

Is there any documentation or learning material about shipment cost in SAP Public Cloud?

Regards

Johan

entry.title: Re: How to set value help for application job parameters
entry.content:
Hi Megumi,

IF_APJ_RT_VALUE_HELP_EXIT is currently only available for On Premise. For the Cloud, this functionality will be released with the next version, 2411, mid of November. 

Best regards, 
Yasmina van Rooij

entry.title: How to overwrite Reset event in sap.m.p13n.Engine
entry.content:
In the sap.m.p13.Engine, is there any way to override the default behaviour of the Reset button in the dialog. All the reset does is change things back to how they were when the dialog was first initiated. However, we want it to reset to be the application defaults. This was possible in the PersoDialog, but does not seem to be possible in the new Engine. We want to stop using PersoDialog as it is deprecated, but functionality that was previously possible, does not seem to be possible anymore.

Engine

Sample: https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/#/entity/sap.m.p13n.Engine/sample/sap.m.sample.p13n.Engine

entry.title: Re: Update values of columns of ALV grid according to the edited field.
entry.content:
TYPES : BEGIN OF ty_likp,
          btgew TYPE gsgew, " total weight
          ntgew TYPE ntgew_15, " net weight
          total TYPE P DECIMALS 3,
        END OF ty_likp.
DATA:gt_fcat TYPE slis_t_fieldcat_alv,
     gs_fcat TYPE slis_fieldcat_alv.
DATA : gt_likp TYPE TABLE OF ty_likp.
DATA: grid TYPE REF TO cl_gui_alv_grid.
DEFINE fcat.
  gs_fcat-col_pos = &1.
  gs_fcat-fieldname = &2.
  gs_fcat-tabname = &3.
  gs_fcat-seltext_l = &4.
  gs_fcat-edit      = &5.
  APPEND gs_fcat TO gt_fcat.
  CLEAR gs_fcat.
END-OF-DEFINITION.
*DATA:GO_REF TYPE REF TO lc_handler.
START-OF-SELECTION.
  SELECT btgew , ntgew FROM likp INTO TABLE @GT_likp.
  LOOP AT gt_likp INTO DATA(ls_likp).
    ls_likp-total = ls_likp-btgew + ls_likp-ntgew.
    MODIFY gt_likp FROM ls_likp.
  ENDLOOP.
  PERFORM display_data.
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Form display_data
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& text
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& -->  p1        text
*& <--  p2        text
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM display_data .
  IF gt_fcat IS INITIAL.
    fcat '1' 'BTGEW' 'GT_LIKP' 'Total weight' ''.
    fcat '2' 'NTGEW' 'GT_LIKP' 'Net Weight' 'X'.
    fcat '3' 'TOTAL' 'GT_LIKP' 'Grand Total' ''.
  ENDIF.
  DATA:it_data TYPE slis_t_event.
  CALL FUNCTION 'REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY'
    EXPORTING
      i_callback_program       = sy-repid
*      i_callback_user_command  = 'USER_CMD'
*      i_callback_pf_status_set = 'ZMVSTATUS'
      i_callback_top_of_page   = 'TOP_OF_PAGE'
*     it_events                = it_data
      it_fieldcat              = gt_fcat
    TABLES
      t_outtab                 = gt_likp
    EXCEPTIONS
      program_error            = 1
      OTHERS                   = 2.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
* Implement suitable error handling here
  ENDIF.
ENDFORM.
FORM top_of_page.
  DATA:
  lo_alv TYPE REF TO cl_gui_alv_grid.
  CALL FUNCTION 'GET_GLOBALS_FROM_SLVC_FULLSCR'
    IMPORTING
      e_grid = lo_alv.
  IF lo_alv IS NOT INITIAL.
    CALL METHOD lo_alv->check_changed_data.
  ENDIF.
  LOOP AT gt_likp ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs>).
    <fs>-total = <fs>-btgew + <fs>-ntgew.
  ENDLOOP .
  CALL METHOD lo_alv->refresh_table_display.
ENDFORM.

Everytime we click on enter Top-of-page event will trigger and refresh the ALV . So that logic we have written inside top-of-page.

entry.title: Re: TCode for generating SLI form
entry.content:
Hello Alekhya,

you have to process the message for SLI in the Export Declaration. you can find it in the 'Communication Tab' of the declaration and then execute or select the message and from the above tool bar you have the option to see the print preview of SLI .

 

Thank You,

Srinivas Yellampalli

entry.title: Re: How to set value help for application job parameters
entry.content:
Hi Megumi,

IF_APJ_RT_VALUE_HELP_EXIT is currently only available for On Premise. For the Cloud, this functionality will be released with the next version, 2411, mid of November. 

Best regards, 
Yasmina van Rooij

entry.title: Re: Fiori launchpad does not recognize my fiori elements extensions
entry.content:
1) How was this extension developed?

2) Are you deploying the app in BTP and why?

3) Are you deploying the app in S/4?

4) Which launchpad? Are you using BTP Build work zone?

entry.title: Re: How to set default values ​​for application job param...
entry.content:
Hi Megumi, 

the function et_parameter_val in if_apj_dt_exec_object~get_parameters is correct, there seems to be a bug if the parameter values are displayed as blank. Please open a ticket on component BC-SRV-APS-APJ so we can check the issue. 

Best regards, 

Yasmina van Rooij

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 1 Contest Activities
entry.content:
Hello Moderator

Have a issue with SAP Crossword, where for one of the question 15th

A useful component in SAP Build Apps under "Display" (10)

I see the answer is from the screen shot attaching from Build apps which is marked yellow, but the answer is showing as not correct. I see some of the people already completed this contest. But I have also cleared the cache and tried with different browser as well,  but the issue not fixed. 

Please let me know if any one faced such? and Any resolution for this to fix from backend.

Thanks

entry.title: Exported the Model did some changes, which is not reflecting when reuploaded.
entry.content:
I am using sap sac tenant. I took standard story from content network [ "Rapid Workforce planning" ], so they were using 2020,2021 and 2022 and did some chnages in dates like just on 2021 is 2023 and on 2023 is 2024. I exported that specific model in excel and make some changes i wanted in that model. Model name is "SAP__HR_IM_EC_WORKFORCE", when i was reimporting in overview of model i am showing the changes. when i use in story still same. I will add necessary screenshot as well. 

 

this is my updated model it is showing in Effective of period and date that changes are applied when but when i am expanding this column the sme dates are giving. and also wheen i apply on sotry filter for this specific column same 2020, 2021 and 2023 coming. 

what can be the solution for it?

entry.title: Recording SAP GUI Dropdown in Enable Now
entry.content:
Hi community, 

While recording a dropdown button like that in GUI - the button doesnt open the dropdown as in attached screenshot. Once the recording is off - the dropdown open normally and is visible. 

How can we record the click - opening of dropdown - and click on of the options so that we have it on record.

Thank you and habe a nice weekend! 🙂

Mira

entry.title: Re: Which Technology SSO implement
entry.content:
Hi,

please see an answer to your question in the following thread:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/which-technology-sso-implement/qaq-p/13799831

Best regards,

Martina

entry.title: Re: Based on value selected using F4 , disable another field
entry.content: Have You Got The Soliution?

entry.title: Re: How to change FIORI App Deploy Task Language in BAS
entry.content: It might be the default language on the user level as well. And if by any chance you use a technical user in the BTP destination you are using to deploy, of course you would check the language of such user.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
entry.content:
The moods 🙂 

#petoberfest #devtoberfest

entry.title: FOTV Stylesheet import
entry.content:
Dear All,

my stylesheet does not show any values, only the heading "TEST" shows up.

  • Transaction FOTV
  • Execute
  • In the menuSettings menu
  • Import stylesheet
  • Upload file ustva_2024 (the file comes from 3399546)

Via STRANS i see transformation FOT00012024DEEDCLXSLT is updated. However

  • FOTV
  • Click Show details of package button
  • Click Show detailed data formatted

Now a form appears, but there is no content altough we do have data, only the heading TEST CASE shows up.

Any idea which part is missing?

Many thanks,

Sebastian

entry.title: Re: Unable to proceed on MDK Annotations
entry.content:
@ABMetallica 

With recent release of MDK editor, the original issue has been fixed. Please test again.

entry.title: Re: Issue during Deploy MDK App in the Dev Space
entry.content:
@Angelo_Ab 

With recent release of MDK editor, the original issue has been fixed. Please test again.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
entry.content: "Oh, really? Petoberfest again? You are going to stage once again some SAP stuff and take a picture? Ok, human, I'll allow... this time".

entry.title: Re: How to name elements in custom root entity in RAP dyn...
entry.content: Short answer: You can't. But... what do you mean by that? Please clarify what you are considering such option
